Task
definitions.doc: A tracked remediation task. Tasks are created from findings across any tab (lint, security, deps, a11y, …) and persisted locally so they survive reloads. The installed CLI never sees these — they're a developer-side worklist layered on top of the analysis.

definitions.name: ScanContext
definitions.doc: Shared, read-once view of the project that every insight collector reuses. The filesystem is walked a single time; file contents are read lazily and cached so multiple collectors scanning the same file pay the cost once.

definitions.name: DocStandard
definitions.doc: One scoring standard contributing to the overall documentation benchmark. We combine the Vercel Agent Readability spec, the llms.txt spec (llmstxt.org), the farming-labs Agent Score, and a general documentation quality rubric. Each yields a 0–100 sub-score; the benchmark is their weighted blend.

definitions.name: ProviderDef
definitions.doc: Provider detection table, in priority order. The first dependency that's present wins. Better Auth has deep introspection; the others are detected and surfaced with provider-level findings and guidance.

definitions.name: ProviderDef
TaskColumn
definitions.doc: A board column (workflow status). Columns are user-customizable — the three defaults (To do / In progress / Done) can be renamed or removed and new ones added. The column flagged `done` counts its tasks as completed.

definitions.name: TaskGroup
definitions.doc: A user-defined grouping (a.k.a. tag / list) tasks can be filed under, e.g. "This sprint", "Tech debt". Issues from any tab can be added to a group.

definitions.name: EnvVariable
definitions.source:
export interface EnvVariable {
  key: string
  scope: EnvScope
  status: EnvStatus
  severity: Severity
  usedIn: string[]
  definedIn: string[]
  note: string
  sample?: string
  /** Raw, unmasked value parsed from the local env file. Read only from the
   *  developer's own machine and revealed behind an explicit toggle. */
  value?: string
  /** Per-file values so the UI can compare .env.local vs .env.example, etc. */
  values?: { file: string; value: string | null }[]
}

definitions.source:
export interface RunState {
  phases: Record<RunPhase, PhaseStatus>
  logs: LogLine[]
  running: boolean
  done: boolean
  activePhase: RunPhase | null
  elapsedMs: number
  start: () => void
  /**
   * How the current run is being driven:
   * - `live` — real events from a connected Projectlens CLI backend
   * - `sim`  — scripted fallback animation (no backend / standalone preview)
   * - `null` — not yet determined
   */
  mode: "live" | "sim" | null
}

  line?: number
  groupId?: string
  note?: string
  createdAt: string

}

definitions.name: DataSource
definitions.source: type DataSource = "live" | "demo" | "empty" | "loading"
definitions.doc: Where the rendered data came from: - `live`   — a real Projectlens CLI backend (via /api/state + /ws) - `demo`   — bundled sample data, loaded on demand from the Run-checks menu - `empty`  — no run yet; everything is zero/empty (the default) - `loading`— resolving the backend

definitions.name: UseDashboardData
definitions.doc: Resolves the dashboard's data. When served by the Projectlens CLI, this fetches the real analysis from `/api/state` and live-updates over the `/ws` socket. In the standalone preview (no CLI backend) it falls back to bundled mock data so the UI is always populated.
